extra.message: User input from request.args.get('search') is concatenated directly into SQL string using f-string and passed to cursor.execute(). This allows SQL injection via the 'search' query parameter on /dashboard.

The 'filename' URL parameter is passed directly to send_from_directory in the /uploads/profile_pictures/<filename> route without authentication or sanitization. Although send_from_directory provides some protection, this endpoint has no authentication allowing any profile picture (numeric ID) to be accessed by unauthenticated users (IDOR).

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The 'filename' URL parameter is concatenated with UPLOAD_FOLDER via os.path.join and passed to os.remove(). A filename containing '../' sequences can escape the uploads directory and delete arbitrary files on the filesystem.

Uploaded file.filename is used directly without sanitization (no secure_filename) and joined with UPLOAD_FOLDER before saving. A malicious filename with '../' sequences can write files outside the upload directory.

The DELETE /api/v1/delete-user/<user_id> endpoint has no authentication or authorization checks (the @token_required decorator is commented out), allowing any unauthenticated user to delete any user account by ID.

The GET /api/v1/profile endpoint accepts a user_id query parameter and returns any user's profile (including role and permissions) with no authentication check (the @token_required decorator is commented out). This is an IDOR vulnerability allowing any unauthenticated user to enumerate user profiles.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: User-supplied picture_url is passed directly to requests.get() with no validation of the URL scheme or host. An attacker can make the server issue arbitrary HTTP requests to internal services (SSRF), including metadata endpoints (e.g., 169.254.169.254) or file:// URLs in some configurations.

The /api/v1/update_picture endpoint has no authentication. It takes a username from the request body and updates that user's profile picture, allowing any unauthenticated attacker to modify any user's profile picture.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as 'just_another_secret'. This same key is used for signing session cookies and JWT tokens (see api_v1.py line 94, 133), allowing attackers who obtain the source code to forge sessions and JWTs.
Flask DEBUG mode is enabled. Debug mode enables the interactive Werkzeug debugger, which allows arbitrary Python code execution via the web interface when exceptions occur.
Passwords are hashed with unsalted MD5 (custom_hash function). MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsalted hashes allow rainbow-table attacks. Use bcrypt/argon2/scrypt with a proper salt.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY is set to False. This makes the session cookie accessible to JavaScript, enabling session hijacking via XSS.

The /update_profile endpoint allows any authenticated user to update their own 'role' and 'permissions' fields via form parameters. An attacker can set role='admin' or permissions='all' to elevate privileges.
The /search endpoint reflects the user-supplied 'query' parameter back into the rendered template. If the template renders this value without escaping (e.g., using |safe), this enables reflected XSS.
The exception message str(e) is returned directly to the client, which can leak sensitive information such as internal file paths, database schema, or environment details.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message

extra.message: The 'filename' URL parameter is used to query the database and passed to send_from_directory. While send_from_directory provides some path traversal protection, the filename stored at upload time (line 262) was never sanitized with secure_filename, so malicious names persist in the DB.
